Probiotics to the rescue: F. prausnitzii and beneficial communities to re-establish motor performance in Parkinson mouse models

Probiotics to the rescue: F. prausnitzii and beneficial communities to re-establish motor performance in Parkinson mouse models

  • 1 Create output directories all in one chunk
  • 2 Prepare data for analysis (run only once)
  • 3 Dataset-wide microbiome composition statistics
    • 3.1 Number of taxa per taxonomic level
    • 3.2 Prevalence distribution of each taxon
    • 3.3 Relative abundance of 30 most abundant bacteria per taxonomic level
      • 3.3.1 Kingdom
      • 3.3.2 Phylum
      • 3.3.3 Class
      • 3.3.4 Order
      • 3.3.5 Family
      • 3.3.6 Genus
      • 3.3.7 Species
      • 3.3.8 SGB
    • 3.4 Heatmap all taxa and samples
  • 4 Analyses were be performed at the SGB level

Anastasia Moiseyenko, Giacomo Antonello, Nicola Segata, Levi Waldron, Sarkis Mazmanian

2025-06-08

knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

1 Create output directories all in one chunk

output_directories.list <- list(
  # alpha diversity directory
  alpha_div_outdir = file.path(params$basic_outdir, params$tax_level, "01_alpha_diversity"),
  # beta diversity output directory
  beta_div_outdir = file.path(params$basic_outdir, params$tax_level, "02_beta_diversity"),
  # differential abundance temporary directory
  maaslin_tmpdir = "~/Documents/maaslin3_tmp_results/mazmanian_matheus_proj4",
  # differential abundance output directory
  maaslin_outdir = file.path(params$basic_outdir, params$tax_level, "03_diff_abund"),
  # motor performance regression
  motorPerformance_outdir = file.path(params$basic_outdir, params$tax_level, "03_diff_abund", "motorPerformance")
)

tmp_out <- capture.output(sapply(output_directories.list, dir.create, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE))
rm(tmp_out)

2 Prepare data for analysis (run only once)

# Load metadata
metadata.df <- read_tsv("Data/metadata_4.0.tsv") %>% 
  # format a few values in the metadata
  mutate(
    genotype = factor(genotype, levels = c("WT", "ASO")),
    cage = as.factor(cage),
    cohort = as.factor(cohort),
    subcohort = as.factor(subcohort),
    treatment = as.factor(treatment) %>% relevel(ref = "Vehicle")
    )

googleCloudStorageR::gcs_auth(json_file = "~/Downloads/curatedmetagenomicdata-232f4a306d1d.json")

# METAPHLAN4

mpa_files_cache <- cacheMetagenomicData(metadata.df$uuid, data_type = "relative_abundance", ask_on_update = FALSE) %>% 
  # retrieve reads per sample too
  mutate(n_reads_processed = get_reads_processed.vec(cache_path))

# metaphlan <- get_metaphlan_locators(metadata.df$uuid, data_type = "relative_abundance")
metaphlan_profiles <- loadMetagenomicData(mpa_files_cache)

# the order of samples is preserved this way
metadata.df <- as.data.frame(dplyr::left_join(metadata.df, select(mpa_files_cache, uuid = UUID, n_reads_processed), by = "uuid"))
rownames(metadata.df) <- metadata.df$uuid
source("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g-antonello/biobakeryUtils/refs/heads/main/R/wrangle_metaphlan.R")
source("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/g-antonello/biobakeryUtils/refs/heads/main/R/complete_unknown_taxonomy.R")

mpa_raw <- cbind(
  as.data.frame(rowData(metaphlan_profiles)) %>%
    rownames_to_column("clade_name"), assay(metaphlan_profiles)) %>% 
  as_tibble() %>% 
  select(-additional_species)

dir.create("Data/metaphlan_profiles/project-4", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
write_tsv(mpa_raw, "Data/metaphlan_profiles/project-4/metaphlan4_data.tsv")


# wrangle metaphlan4 data
mpa4_wrangled_taxonomy_mpa <- assay(metaphlan_profiles) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column("clade_name") %>% 
  wrangle_metaphlan(taxonomic_lvl = params$tax_level)

InputData.tse <- TreeSummarizedExperiment(
  rowData = mpa4_wrangled_taxonomy_mpa$taxonomies,
  colData = metadata.df,
  assays = list(
    metaphlan_RelAbund = as.matrix(mpa4_wrangled_taxonomy_mpa$profiles/100)[,rownames(metadata.df)],
    metaphlan_Percent = as.matrix(mpa4_wrangled_taxonomy_mpa$profiles)[,rownames(metadata.df)]
    )
)
saveRDS(InputData.tse, "Data/InputData.tse.Rds")
InputData.tse <- readRDS("Data/InputData.tse.Rds")
InputData.tse
## class: TreeSummarizedExperiment 
## dim: 463 48 
## metadata(0):
## assays(2): metaphlan_RelAbund metaphlan_Percent
## rownames(463): UNCLASSIFIED t__SGB41723 ... t__SGB41413 t__SGB42316
## rowData names(8): Kingdom Phylum ... Species SGB
## colnames(48): 5c7657ee-666d-4545-a5f4-e3b97972700c
##   ef1f9867-c5fc-4ba0-838d-848b58467b10 ...
##   4a571d8f-049d-4057-8034-883f1fc99ab2
##   700a1335-1157-4209-88da-172a9d744849
## colData names(37): sample_name library_ID ... study_name
##   n_reads_processed
## reducedDimNames(0):
## mainExpName: NULL
## altExpNames(0):
## rowLinks: NULL
## rowTree: NULL
## colLinks: NULL
## colTree: NULL

3 Dataset-wide microbiome composition statistics

3.1 Number of taxa per taxonomic level

InputData_splitByRanks.tse <- append(splitByRanks(InputData.tse), list("SGB" = InputData.tse))
 
InputData_splitByRanks.tse %>%  
  sapply(nrow)
## Kingdom  Phylum   Class   Order  Family   Genus Species     SGB 
##       2      10      83      87     103     299     396     463

3.2 Prevalence distribution of each taxon

InputData_splitByRanks.tse %>% 
  lapply(assay) %>% 
  lapply(apply, 1, function(x) sum(x > 0)) %>% 
  sapply(summary)
##         Kingdom Phylum    Class    Order   Family   Genus  Species      SGB
## Min.         48    3.0  1.00000  1.00000  1.00000  1.0000  1.00000  1.00000
## 1st Qu.      48   48.0  8.50000  7.50000 10.00000 10.0000  9.00000 10.00000
## Median       48   48.0 39.00000 39.00000 41.00000 33.0000 31.00000 32.00000
## Mean         48   43.5 29.81928 29.57471 31.65049 28.8194 27.60606 28.48812
## 3rd Qu.      48   48.0 48.00000 48.00000 48.00000 46.0000 46.00000 46.00000
## Max.         48   48.0 48.00000 48.00000 48.00000 48.0000 48.00000 48.00000

3.3 Relative abundance of 30 most abundant bacteria per taxonomic level

tmp <- InputData_splitByRanks.tse %>% 
  lapply(function(tse){
    max_n <- nrow(tse)
    top30MostAbund <- getTop(tse, assay.type = "metaphlan_RelAbund", method = "mean", top = min(c(30, max_n)))
    tse.subset <- tse[top30MostAbund,]
    return(tse.subset)
  })
for (tx in names(tmp)) {
  # Create header with cat()
  cat("\n\n###", tx, "\n")
  
  # Create plot and convert to plotly
  p <- miaViz::plotAbundance(tmp[[tx]], 
                            assay.type = "metaphlan_RelAbund",
                            group = tx) +
       facet_grid(cols = vars(cohort, treatment), scales = "free_x") + 
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  # Explicitly print plotly object
  print(p)
  
  cat("\n")
}

3.3.1 Kingdom

3.3.2 Phylum

3.3.3 Class

3.3.4 Order

3.3.5 Family

3.3.6 Genus

3.3.7 Species

3.3.8 SGB

This is again to show that there are too many taxa to easily visualize them. We need to rely on other methods.

3.4 Heatmap all taxa and samples

pheatmap::pheatmap(assay(InputData.tse) %>% magrittr::set_colnames(colData(InputData.tse)$sample_name), scale = "row", annotation_col = colData(InputData.tse) %>% as.data.frame() %>%  magrittr::set_rownames(.$sample_name) %>% select(treatment, cohort), angle_col = 315, show_rownames = FALSE)

Figure 3.1: Heatmap of full microbiome composition of the dataset. Relative abundances have been Z-scaled by taxon, to account for intrinsic differences in relative abundances in the microbiome

4 Analyses were be performed at the SGB level

Below the TreeSummarizedExperiment object summary that is going to be used in all following analyses

InputData.tse <- InputData_splitByRanks.tse[[params$tax_level]]

InputData.tse
